_template_instructions.purpose: Assess ISO 14971 Annex C safety characteristics to systematically identify hazards
_template_instructions.when_to_fill: After reviewing the system's intended use and clinical context
_template_instructions.how_to_use: 1. Copy to Output/<system>/system/defined/safety-characteristics.json, 2. Assess each characteristic, 3. Remove _template_instructions
references: ISO 14971:2019 Annex C - Questions that can be used to identify medical device characteristics

safety_characteristics.guidance_question: What is the intended use and how is the medical device to be used?
identified_hazards: <FILL: hazard IDs identified through this characteristic>
safety_characteristics._notes: This is foundational - must be completed first

safety_characteristics.applicability_rationale: <FILL: What measurements, if any? e.g., Timing calculations, scheduling>
safety_characteristics._examples: Timing calculations, dose calculations, vital sign measurements

safety_characteristics.applicability_rationale: <FILL: Does the system interpret data and provide conclusions? e.g., Escalation rules interpret clinical urgency>
safety_characteristics._examples: Clinical decision support, triage algorithms, escalation rules

safety_characteristics.guidance_question: Is the medical device intended for use in conjunction with other medical devices, medicines or other medical technologies?
safety_characteristics.applicability_rationale: <FILL: What integrations? e.g., EHR integration, booking systems>
safety_characteristics._examples: EHR integration, lab systems, pharmacy systems, other medical devices

safety_characteristics.guidance_question: Are there unwanted outputs of energy or substances?
Is the medical device susceptible to environmental influences?
safety_characteristics._examples: Network outages, cloud service availability, database failures

safety_characteristics.guidance_question: Does the medical device contain software?
safety_characteristics._notes: Always applicable for software systems. Link to all software failure modes.

safety_characteristics.guidance_question: Does the medical device have a data entry function?
safety_characteristics.applicability_rationale: <FILL: What data is entered? e.g., Appointment details, clinical notes>
safety_characteristics._examples: Appointment scheduling, clinical notes, patient information entry
Web UI, mobile app, practitioner portal

safety_characteristics.guidance_question: Does safe use of the medical device depend on training?
safety_characteristics.applicability_rationale: <FILL: What training is needed for safe use?>
safety_characteristics._examples: Workflow training, system operation training, clinical interpretation training

safety_characteristics.guidance_question: Does the medical device introduce new technologies or application methods?
safety_characteristics.applicability_rationale: <FILL: Any novel approaches? AI/ML? New workflows?>
safety_characteristics._examples: AI-based triage, novel algorithms, unfamiliar workflows

safety_characteristics.guidance_question: Is successful application of the medical device critically dependent on human factors such as the user interface?
safety_characteristics.applicability_rationale: <FILL: Critical UI interactions? e.g., Handover confirmation, escalation acknowledgment>
safety_characteristics._examples: Confirmation dialogs, critical data display, workflow steps requiring user action

safety_characteristics.guidance_question: Does the medical device use an alarm system?
safety_characteristics._examples: Escalation notifications, appointment reminders, critical alerts

safety_characteristics.guidance_question: In what ways might the medical device be deliberately misused?
safety_characteristics._examples: Unauthorized access, data manipulation, identity spoofing

safety_characteristics.guidance_question: Does the medical device hold data critical to patient care?
safety_characteristics.applicability_rationale: <FILL: What critical data? e.g., Appointment data, clinical notes, care coordination>
safety_characteristics._examples: Appointment schedules, clinical notes, referral information, escalation status

safety_characteristics.guidance_question: Is the medical device intended to be a single fault condition or a component in a single fault condition?
safety_characteristics.applicability_rationale: <FILL: Is this the only barrier? Redundancy?>
safety_characteristics._examples: System is sole source of truth for appointments, no paper backup

safety_characteristics.guidance_question: Does the use of the medical device depend on essential performance?
safety_characteristics.applicability_rationale: <FILL: What functions must work reliably for patient safety? e.g., Escalation must trigger>
safety_characteristics._examples: Escalation rules must evaluate correctly, notifications must be delivered
